summary.intent: Implement task #286 — a framework-agnostic state machine (Table agnostic core) for the Table data-display component in the ars-ui Rust library, including spec drift fixes, full test coverage, and a mandatory Codex review loop until 👍 approval.
summary.outcome: Implementation completed: ~1843 LOC state machine with 133 tests and 37 snapshot fixtures. Six rounds of Codex review addressed 28 findings; all threads replied, resolved, and rebased. Final push for the last finding (SetDirection Auto resolution) was in progress at transcript end.

repo: Tabs (`crates/ars-components/src/navigation/tabs/`) is the canonical structural template for complex stateful components — same Machine/State/Context/Props/Api/ConnectApi layout.

`Bindable<T>::set()` on a controlled Bindable does NOT change what `get()` returns; internal state (e.g., selection_state.selected_keys) must always be re-synced from `get()` after any `sync_controlled` call.
`selection::State::toggle` requires a Collection generic that the agnostic Table core doesn't own — toggle must be computed manually via `is_selected + select/deselect`.

`Set::All` cannot be individually deselected without materialization; `AllVisible` materializes to `Multiple(ctx.rows)` on individual deselect, while `AllData` preserves `Set::All` — BulkSelection handles exclusions at adapter layer (§5.2).
Non-sortable column headers MUST omit `aria-sort` entirely per §3.4.2 — setting `aria-sort='none'` unconditionally is a spec bug (named test locks this).
`Direction::Auto` must be resolved to a concrete direction via `locale.direction()` at both `init` time and `SetDirection` time — storing Auto in ctx breaks RTL detection.
`on_props_changed` must be overridden explicitly to dispatch `SyncProps`/`SyncControlled*`/`SetDirection` events — the framework default is a no-op and prop changes won't propagate otherwise.
Spec/code drift must be resolved in the same PR per CLAUDE.md; this component had 11+ internal spec inconsistencies that were fixed alongside the code.
Proptest suites are marked `#[ignore = 'proptest — nightly extended-proptest job']` by workspace convention; they run in a separate nightly job, not normal CI.
90% line coverage is the crate-level xci-fast threshold; file-level branch coverage can dip below if crate-level passes.
The `snapshot-reviewed` label is required on PRs creating `.snap` files. Codex review is triggered via `@codex review` comment; CLAUDE.md mandates waiting for 👍 before merge.
is_sorting should be adapter-controlled via a dedicated `SetIsSorting(bool)` event rather than set implicitly inside SortColumn — SortColumn runs before async sort resolves, leaving the flag stuck.

code.finding: SetDirection arm must resolve Direction::Auto via ctx.locale.direction() before storing — auto stored in ctx silently breaks on_cell_keydown's RTL detection which checks == Direction::Rtl.
is_row_selected must short-circuit to false for disabled keys even when selection is Set::All; all_selected/some_selected must also filter disabled_keys — otherwise disabled rows appear selected.
SyncProps must walk column_widths and re-clamp every entry when min_column_width changes — only clamping new ColumnResize events leaves previously-written widths stale.
Part::ColumnHeader needs a `sortable: bool` field so ConnectApi::part_attrs honors caller intent rather than hardcoding sortable=true for every column header dispatch.

SyncControlledSortDescriptor payload must be Option<Option<SortDescriptor<String>>> — the outer None exits controlled mode entirely; Option<SortDescriptor> alone can only change the value, not toggle controlled state.
SetRows must rebase focused_cell even when focused_row is None — focused_cell.row_index can go out of bounds after rows are removed regardless of whether focused_row was set.
table_attrs must emit aria-multiselectable='true' when interactive && selection_mode == Multiple per ARIA grid spec.
ConnectApi::part_attrs for Part::SelectAllCheckbox must build Vec<&Key> from ctx.rows — passing an empty slice always reports zero selected rows regardless of actual selection state.
Part::ColumnHeader anatomy entry requires sortable: bool field; spec_conformance test must pass sortable: false to exercise the correct non-sortable anatomy path.

workflow: Write failing tests first (TDD red) for every Codex finding before implementing — provides regression guard and validates understanding of the bug before touching production code.
Run cargo fmt before xci-fast on every push — formatter failures block the gate and are easy to overlook after edits.
Commit before rebasing when main has advanced — commit first, then rebase, then force-push the rebased branch.
Background polling scripts in /tmp are fragile across context windows — always re-verify PR state directly with gh CLI when a poller exits with code 127.
Resolve all Codex threads (not just reply) before re-triggering with @codex review — unresolved threads from prior passes reappear in the next summary.
Post-implementation audit catches real drift even after careful spec reading — Phase 1 found 11 items, Phases 2–3 found another 5.
cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject is the correct gate for snapshot hygiene; it catches leftover .snap files from deleted tests.
Batching task tracking (one TaskCreate per Codex finding, TaskUpdate on resolution) keeps a multi-round review loop organized without polluting the main context.

friction: API socket error ('socket connection was closed unexpectedly') interrupted plan writing mid-draft, requiring a user prompt to resume and rewrite.
Context/rate limit hit mid-session ('You've hit your limit'), requiring a second 'continue from where you left off' prompt from the user.
Background polling scripts in /tmp were wiped between turns (exit code 127) twice, requiring manual PR state re-verification and script recreation each time.

Six rounds of Codex review with 28 compounding findings — each round introduced edge cases exposed by previous fixes (e.g., AllData/AllVisible semantics changed the materialize contract, breaking round-2 toggle tests).
selection::State::toggle requires a Collection generic the agnostic Table core doesn't own — toggle had to be reimplemented manually via is_selected + select/deselect.
Clippy repeatedly flagged *id derefs, unused mut, and needless_if patterns requiring extra cleanup passes after each major edit round.
open_items: ScrollToRow(Key) event and full Virtualizer integration are explicitly deferred — requires widening Effect from NoEffect to a typed enum and pulling in ars-collections::Virtualizer plumbing.
BulkSelection exclusion-set wrapper (§5.2) is intentionally excluded — it is adapter/application code layered on top of Set::All; the agnostic core does not model it.
on_load_more: Option<Callback<()>> callback is adapter-only and not stored in the core — adapter PRs must wire it.
The final Codex finding (T49S — SetDirection(Auto) resolution) was implemented and all 133 tests passed, but the commit/push/thread-resolve cycle was still in progress at transcript end.
File-level branch coverage on table/mod.rs sits at ~89-90% (at or just below threshold) — keyboard navigation helpers and AllData guard branches are the main uncovered paths.
PR body must include 'Closes #286' and the snapshot-reviewed label must be confirmed applied before merge — both were noted in the plan but not explicitly verified as done.
